sections.heading: Surveillance
sections.content:
Physical exam (including pelvic exam) every 3-6 months for 2-3 years, then every 6-12 months for up to year 5, then annually [20].
For uterine sarcoma: Consider H&P every 3-4 months for 2-3 years, then every 6-12 months [21].
Symptom assessment for potential recurrence (bleeding, pain, weight loss, respiratory symptoms) [20].

Imaging should be guided by symptoms and clinical suspicion, not performed routinely in asymptomatic patients [20].
For endometrial cancer: Consider chest imaging if symptoms or high-risk features; imaging as indicated for suspected recurrence [22].
For uterine sarcoma: Chest/abdomen/pelvis CT every 3-6 months for 3 years, then every 6-12 months for 2 years; optional imaging beyond 5 years depending on histology and stage [21].
FDG-PET/CT may be considered to clarify ambiguous findings [22].

CA-125 if initially elevated or for serous histology to monitor response [20].
Routine tumor markers are not recommended for all patients [20].

Health maintenance: blood pressure, breast exam, mammography, stool guaiac, immunizations as appropriate [18].
Counseling on lifestyle: exercise, smoking cessation, nutrition, and weight management [18].
Assessment of late effects of treatment and referral to specialists (e.g., pelvic floor therapy, sexual health, psychotherapy) [18].
Survivorship care plan summarizing treatment and follow-up recommendations [18].

timeline.title: Every 3–6 months for 2–3 years (Early follow-up)
timeline.body: Physical exam (including pelvic exam) every 3–6 months. For uterine sarcoma: H&P every 3–4 months. Symptom assessment for potential recurrence (bleeding, pain, weight loss, respiratory symptoms). For uterine sarcoma: Chest/abdomen/pelvis CT every 3–6 months. CA-125 if initially elevated or for serous histology to monitor response. Health maintenance (blood pressure, breast exam, mammography, stool guaiac, immunizations) as appropriate. Counsel on lifestyle (exercise, smoking cessation, nutrition, weight management). Begin assessment of late effects and referral to specialists as needed. Survivorship care plan.

timeline.title: Every 6–12 months for up to year 5 (Intermediate follow-up)
timeline.body: Physical exam (including pelvic exam) every 6–12 months. For uterine sarcoma: H&P every 6–12 months. Symptom assessment for potential recurrence. For uterine sarcoma: Chest/abdomen/pelvis CT every 6–12 months for up to 5 years (cumulative). CA-125 if initially elevated or for serous histology. Continue health maintenance and counseling. For endometrial cancer: Consider chest imaging if symptoms or high-risk features; imaging as indicated for suspected recurrence. FDG-PET/CT may be considered to clarify ambiguous findings. Continue assessment of late effects and referrals. Update survivorship care plan.

timeline.title: Annually after 5 years (Long-term follow-up)
timeline.body: Physical exam annually. Symptom assessment for potential recurrence. For uterine sarcoma: optional imaging beyond 5 years depending on histology and stage. CA-125 if indicated. Annual health maintenance: blood pressure, breast exam, mammography, stool guaiac, immunizations. Counsel on lifestyle (exercise, smoking cessation, nutrition, weight management). Assessment of late effects and referral to specialists (pelvic floor therapy, sexual health, psychotherapy). Survivorship care plan summarizing treatment and follow-up recommendations.
